textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: Sound is produced through vibrations that occur from a sound source.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: Sound waves can also interfere with each other when they meet.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: Destructive interference occurs when a peak of one wave overlaps with a valley of another.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: Constructive interference occurs when the peaks of two waves overlap.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: This technology is effective for predictable sounds like those of car engines.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: It's less effective for inconsistent sounds such as those of people talking close to you.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: Noise can be unpleasant and distracting.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: When peaks of two waves overlap, the amplitude increases.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: The circuitry analyzes the frequency of outside sounds.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: Constructive interference occurs when the peaks of two waves overlap.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: Destructive interference occurs when a peak overlaps with a valley.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: When the peaks of two waves overlap, it results in a bigger wave.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: The circuitry will generate an opposite noise of –1.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation
textbook_words.example: The circuitry analyzes the sounds to produce opposite sound waves.

textbook_words.lesson_subtitle: Tuning Out: The Science of Noise-Cancellation

textbook_words.example: Reducing unwanted disturbances, allowing people to lead more peaceful lives.

textbook_grammar.sentence: wolves, who share the same common ancestor
textbook_grammar.explanation: 선행사(wolves)가 사람/동물일 때 주격 관계대명사 who를 사용. 관계절이 선행사를 수식함.
textbook_grammar.question: 다음 문장에서 관계대명사절을 찾고, 선행사를 쓰시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: who share the same common ancestor / 선행사: wolves

textbook_grammar.sentence: paying no attention to his gestures
textbook_grammar.explanation: 분사구문은 접속사+주어를 생략하고 동사를 현재분사로 변환. 여기서는 '~하면서'(동시상황)의 의미.
textbook_grammar.question: 다음 분사구문을 접속사를 사용한 절로 바꾸시오: 'paying no attention to his gestures'
textbook_grammar.answer: as they paid no attention to his gestures (while / as 사용 가능)

textbook_grammar.sentence: It's fascinating how, in situations like this, we want to help someone in need.
textbook_grammar.explanation: It은 가주어이고, how절이 진주어. 'It ~ that/how/whether + 절' 구조.
textbook_grammar.question: 다음 문장에서 가주어와 진주어를 찾으시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: 가주어: It / 진주어: how ~ in need

textbook_grammar.sentence: Without these characteristics, they could have faced extinction.
textbook_grammar.explanation: could have p.p.는 '~했을 수도 있었다'는 과거에 대한 가능성/추측을 나타냄. Without 가정법과 함께 자주 쓰임.
textbook_grammar.key_pattern: Without + 명사, 주어 + could have p.p.
textbook_grammar.question: 'could have faced'가 나타내는 의미를 우리말로 쓰시오.

textbook_grammar.sentence: Dr. Hare's team set up a device which required two individuals to pull both ends of a rope
textbook_grammar.explanation: 관계대명사 which는 선행사(a device)를 수식. 'require + 목적어 + to부정사' 구조도 함께 확인.
textbook_grammar.question: 밑줄 친 which의 선행사를 쓰시오: 'a device which required two individuals to pull'

textbook_grammar.sentence: For some time, my grandfather had been busy writing down the village genealogy.
textbook_grammar.explanation: had been + V-ing는 과거완료 진행형. 과거의 특정 시점 이전부터 계속된 동작을 나타냄.
textbook_grammar.question: 'had been busy writing'을 우리말로 해석하시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: 족보를 적느라 바빠 왔었다 (과거 이전부터 계속 바빴음)
textbook_grammar.lesson_title: Open a Book, Open the World

textbook_grammar.sentence: He asked, 'Can you take a week off?' → He asked if I could take a week off.
textbook_grammar.explanation: 직접화법을 간접화법으로 전환할 때: 의문문은 if/whether 사용, 시제는 한 단계 과거로 이동.
textbook_grammar.question: 'Can you take a week off?'를 간접화법으로 바꾸시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: He asked if I could take a week off.
textbook_grammar.lesson_title: Open a Book, Open the World

textbook_grammar.sentence: Although the two are genetically similar, they are different in nature.
textbook_grammar.explanation: although/though는 '비록 ~이지만'의 양보 접속사. 주절과 반대되는 내용을 이끔.
textbook_grammar.question: 다음 두 문장을 although를 써서 한 문장으로 연결하시오: 'He was sick. He came to school.'
textbook_grammar.answer: Although he was sick, he came to school.
textbook_grammar.lesson_title: Open a Book, Open the World

textbook_grammar.sentence: I heard my grandfather say faintly, 'I need your help.'
textbook_grammar.explanation: 지각동사(hear, see, feel 등) + 목적어 + 동사원형/V-ing. 동사원형은 동작 전체, V-ing는 진행 중인 동작.
textbook_grammar.question: 'I heard him say'에서 say 대신 saying을 쓰면 의미가 어떻게 달라지는가?
textbook_grammar.answer: say: 말하는 것 전체를 들었다 / saying: 말하는 중인 것을 들었다
textbook_grammar.lesson_title: Open a Book, Open the World

textbook_grammar.sentence: Sound is produced through vibrations that occur from a sound source.
textbook_grammar.explanation: 수동태: be동사 + 과거분사. 행위의 대상이 주어가 됨. by + 행위자는 생략 가능.
textbook_grammar.question: 'Scientists invented noise-cancelling technology.'를 수동태로 바꾸시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: Noise-cancelling technology was invented by scientists.

textbook_grammar.sentence: Sound waves can interfere with each other when they meet. There are two types of interference that scientists have discovered.
textbook_grammar.explanation: 관계대명사 that은 사람/사물 모두에 사용 가능. 목적격 관계대명사는 생략 가능.
textbook_grammar.question: 다음 문장에서 관계대명사 that을 which로 바꿀 수 있는지 판단하시오: 'It is the technology that changed the world.'
textbook_grammar.answer: 바꿀 수 있다. (사물 선행사이므로 which 가능. 단, 선행사가 최상급/서수/all 등이면 that 선호)

textbook_grammar.sentence: This cancels out the unwanted sound, so you can hear the music sound clearly.
textbook_grammar.explanation: so that + 주어 + can/could + 동사원형: '~할 수 있도록'의 목적을 나타냄.
textbook_grammar.key_pattern: 주절 + so that + 주어 + can + 동사원형
textbook_grammar.question: 'We use technology so that we can live more comfortably.'를 우리말로 해석하시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: 우리는 더 편안하게 살 수 있도록 기술을 사용한다.

textbook_grammar.sentence: Instantly transmit the opposite sound to the speakers as soon as the noise reaches the microphones.
textbook_grammar.explanation: as soon as: '~하자마자' 시간의 부사절. 미래의 일에도 현재시제 사용.
textbook_grammar.key_pattern: 주절 + as soon as + 주어 + 동사(현재형)
textbook_grammar.question: '비가 그치자마자 우리는 출발했다'를 as soon as를 이용해 영작하시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: We left as soon as it stopped raining.

textbook_grammar.sentence: Inside the headphones are microphones and noise-cancelling circuitry.
textbook_grammar.explanation: 장소 부사구가 문두에 오면 주어와 동사가 도치됨. '헤드폰 안에 마이크와 회로가 있다'
textbook_grammar.question: 'The answer lies inside the box.'를 도치문으로 바꾸시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: Inside the box lies the answer.

textbook_grammar.sentence: Methane is approximately 25 times more potent than CO2.
textbook_grammar.explanation: 배수 비교: 배수사 + as 형용사 as 또는 배수사 + 비교급 + than. '~배만큼 ~하다'
textbook_grammar.key_pattern: 배수사 + as + 원급 + as / 배수사 + 비교급 + than
textbook_grammar.question: '메탄은 CO2보다 약 25배 더 강력하다'를 as ~ as 구문으로 영작하시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: Methane is approximately 25 times as potent as CO2.

textbook_grammar.sentence: The resulting SCGs are sold to fertilizer companies, where they are transformed into organic fertilizer.
textbook_grammar.explanation: 관계부사 where는 선행사가 장소일 때 사용. 계속적 용법(콤마+where)은 '그리고 거기서'의 의미.
textbook_grammar.question: 밑줄 친 where의 선행사를 쓰고, where를 전치사+which로 바꾸시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: 선행사: fertilizer companies / in which (at which도 가능)

textbook_grammar.sentence: Fabric made from coffee grounds absorbs sweat, dries quickly, and provides UV protection.
textbook_grammar.explanation: 과거분사가 명사를 수식할 때 수동/완료의 의미. 'made from coffee grounds'가 'Fabric'을 수식.
textbook_grammar.question: '커피 찌꺼기로 만들어진 컵'을 영어로 쓰시오.

textbook_grammar.grammar_point: not only A but also B
textbook_grammar.sentence: Reusable cups from coffee grounds not only have a visually appealing appearance but also preserve the taste of the coffee.
textbook_grammar.explanation: not only A but also B: 'A뿐만 아니라 B도'. 상관접속사로 A와 B는 문법적으로 동일한 형태여야 함.
textbook_grammar.key_pattern: not only + A + but also + B
textbook_grammar.question: '그는 영리할 뿐만 아니라 친절하기도 하다'를 not only ~ but also를 사용해 영작하시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: He is not only intelligent but also kind.

textbook_grammar.sentence: By repurposing coffee grounds in this manner, related businesses and local farmers can benefit.
textbook_grammar.explanation: by + V-ing: '~함으로써' 수단이나 방법을 나타냄. 문두에 오면 주절의 주어와 일치해야 함.
textbook_grammar.question: '열심히 공부함으로써 그는 시험에 합격했다'를 by V-ing를 사용해 영작하시오.
textbook_grammar.answer: By studying hard, he passed the exam.
